attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a clean tea pot on the stove.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

1. Objects and their types:
   - Agent: A robotic agent is present in the scene, though not visually depicted, it is the actor performing tasks.
   - Receptacles:
     - Stove: A black stovetop with a metallic control panel and three visible knobs. It is a receptacle because it can hold cookware. It is not openable.
     - Sink: A double-basin stainless steel sink with a faucet. It is a receptacle and is not openable. It is clean and empty.
     - Countertop: A white countertop surface adjacent to the stove and sink. It is not defined as a receptacle in the domain, so it is treated as a regular object.
   - Objects:
     - Tea Pot: A metallic, silver-colored tea pot with a blue handle and a spout. It is currently sitting on the stovetop. It is not clean (as per the instruction, it needs to be cleaned).
     - Fork: A metallic fork lying on the white countertop. It is not relevant to the task but is present in the scene.
     - Pot: A dark-colored pot with a handle, partially visible on the left side of the stove. It is not relevant to the current instruction.

2. Quantities:
   - 1 Agent (robotic, implied)
   - 1 Stove (receptacle)
   - 1 Sink (receptacle)
   - 1 Tea Pot (object)
   - 1 Fork (object)
   - 1 Pot (object, partially visible)

3. Spatial relationships:
   - The tea pot is on the stove (inReceptacle tea_pot stove is true).
   - The fork is on the countertop (not in a receptacle defined in the domain).
   - The stove is adjacent to the countertop.
   - The sink is to the right of the stove and countertop area.
   - The agent is not currently at any object’s location (initial state).

4. Current state of each object relevant to the planning task:
   - Tea Pot: Currently on the stove. It is not clean (isClean tea_pot is false). It is not being held by the agent. It is not hot or cooled.
   - Stove: Not toggled (isOn stove is false). It is not openable.
   - Sink: Not openable. It is empty and clean.
   - Fork: Not relevant to the task. It is on the countertop and not held by the agent.

5. Colors and visual properties:
   - Tea Pot: Metallic silver body with a blue handle and a curved spout. It has a rounded, bulbous shape and sits on the right burner of the stove.
   - Stove: Black stovetop surface with a metallic control panel and three white knobs. The oven door is below with a glass window.
   - Sink: Stainless steel, double-basin, with a high-arc faucet. The basins are empty and reflective.
   - Countertop: White, smooth surface. The fork is placed diagonally on it.
   - Pot: Dark gray or black, with a long handle, partially visible on the left burner of the stove.
   - Walls: Beige textured walls in the background.
   - Cabinets: Wooden cabinets below the sink and stove, with metallic knobs.

Note: The instruction requires putting a clean tea pot on the stove. Currently, the tea pot is already on the stove but is not clean. Therefore, the robot must first pick up the tea pot, clean it in the sink, and then place it back on the stove. The sink is accessible and ready for cleaning. The stove is not currently on (isOn stove is false), but that is not required for this task.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem clean_teapot_on_stove)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        stove - receptacle
        sink - receptacle
        tea_pot - object
        fork - object
        pot - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation robot stove))
        (not (atLocation robot sink))
        (not (atLocation robot tea_pot))
        (not (atLocation robot fork))
        (not (atLocation robot pot))
        (inReceptacle tea_pot stove)
        (not (isClean tea_pot))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (holds robot tea_pot))
        (not (opened stove))
        (not (opened sink))
        (not (isOn stove))
        (not (isHot tea_pot))
        (not (isCool tea_pot))
        (not (isSliced tea_pot))
        (not (isToggled stove))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle tea_pot stove)
        (isClean tea_pot)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem clean_teapot_on_stove)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        stove - receptacle
        sink - receptacle
        tea_pot - object
        fork - object
        pot - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation robot stove))
        (not (atLocation robot sink))
        (not (atLocation robot tea_pot))
        (not (atLocation robot fork))
        (not (atLocation robot pot))
        (inReceptacle tea_pot stove)
        (not (isClean tea_pot))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (holds robot tea_pot))
        (not (opened stove))
        (not (opened sink))
        (not (isOn stove))
        (not (isHot tea_pot))
        (not (isCool tea_pot))
        (not (isSliced tea_pot))
        (not (isToggled stove))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle tea_pot stove)
        (isClean tea_pot)
    ))
)

Scene Description:

1. Objects and Their Types:
   - Agent: A robotic agent is present in the scene but not yet positioned at any specific object location.
   - Receptacles:
     - Stove: A black stovetop with a metallic front panel and three visible control knobs. It is a receptacle capable of holding objects (e.g., pots, kettles). It is not openable.

     - Fork: A metallic fork resting on the white countertop to the right of the stove. It is an object but not relevant to the current task.
     - Pot: A dark-colored pot with a long handle, partially visible on the left burner of the stove. It is an object and not a receptacle in this context.

2. Quantities:
   - 1 Agent (robot)
   - 2 Receptacles: 1 Stove, 1 Sink
   - 3 Objects: 1 Tea Pot, 1 Fork, 1 Pot

(define (problem put_clean_teapot_on_stove)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent - agent
        stove - receptacle
        sink - sink
        tea_pot - object

    )

            (isClean tea_pot)
        )
    )
)

    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )

final.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_clean_teapot_on_stove)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent - agent
        stove - receptacle
        sink - sink
        tea_pot - object
        fork - object
        pot - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation agent stove))
        (not (atLocation agent sink))
        (not (atLocation agent tea_pot))
        (not (atLocation agent fork))
        (not (atLocation agent pot))
        
        ; Tea pot is initially on the stove
        (inReceptacle tea_pot stove)
        
        ; Stove and sink are not openable
        (not (openable stove))
        (not (openable sink))
        
        ; Agent is not holding anything
        (not (holdsAny agent))
        
        ; Tea pot is not clean
        (not (isClean tea_pot))
        
        ; Stove is not toggled on
        (not (isOn stove))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle tea_pot stove)
            (isClean tea_pot)
        )
    )
)
```
final.problem:
(define (problem put_clean_teapot_on_stove)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent - agent
        stove - receptacle
        sink - sink
        tea_pot - object
        fork - object
        pot - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation agent stove))
        (not (atLocation agent sink))
        (not (atLocation agent tea_pot))
        (not (atLocation agent fork))
        (not (atLocation agent pot))
        
        ; Tea pot is initially on the stove
        (inReceptacle tea_pot stove)
        
        ; Stove and sink are not openable
        (not (openable stove))
        (not (openable sink))
        
        ; Agent is not holding anything
        (not (holdsAny agent))
        
        ; Tea pot is not clean
        (not (isClean tea_pot))
        
        ; Stove is not toggled on
        (not (isOn stove))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle tea_pot stove)
            (isClean tea_pot)
        )
    )
)
